subject: [RFC PATCH v2 2/7] mm, swap: support zswap and zeroswap as vswap backends

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the first patch was unnecessary and could be merged into the second patch due to its large size, but did not commit to squashing it.

Author acknowledged a minor issue about inconsistent indentation in the code and clarified that it only occurs when a specific condition is met.

Author questioned whether THP swap in is necessary for non-sync IO devices and considered removing the code, but decided to keep it separate from the current patch for easier review.

Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to handle vswap backends differently and agreed to add a helper function for this purpose, indicating that a fix is planned.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah this comment belongs to the first patch. I added it after the
fact but commit to the second patch.

TBH, the first patch kinda not do much. It just declares a new special
struct swap_info_struct, with some helpers and checks, but it's not
hooked to any allocation path. Logically it should be squashed into
this patch, but this patch is already 600 LoC, lol.

---

Yeah that's just me avoiding indentation lol. But yes, it only happens
in !ret case:

---

I was thinking of removing it altogether haha. Are we even doing THP
swap in for non-sync IO devices?

if (!folio) {
    /* Swapin bypasses readahead for SWP_SYNCHRONOUS_IO devices */
    if (data_race(si->flags & SWP_SYNCHRONOUS_IO))
        folio = swapin_sync(entry, GFP_HIGHUSER_MOVABLE,
[...]
else
    folio = swapin_readahead(entry, GFP_HIGHUSER_MOVABLE, vmf);

So I guess it's primarily zram that does THP swap in here? on
non-SWP_SYNCHRONOUS_IO devices, seems like we only do "THP swapin" if
we catch the page in swap cache (minor page fault). :) Will zram users
like vswap?

OTOH, zswap might be getting THP zswap-in support soon, so it's not
just zram backend that cares about these kinds of check? :)

Or maybe I can keep it, but separate it from this big patch to make it
easier to review :) Lemme play with it.

---

Most just not repeating the vtable lookup-and-lock and what not. :)
The pattern is repeated the third time in swapoff when I allow phys
swap to be the backend of vswap in the next patch so I figure probably
should add some helper.

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch adds support for zswap and zeroswap as virtual swap backends, allowing pages to be compressed or detected as zero-filled without pre-allocating physical swap slots. It builds on top of the existing swap-table infrastructure and introduces virtual swap entries that decouple PTE swap entries from physical backing. The patch only supports zswap and zero-page backends for now, with physical disk backing fallback and writeback support to be added in subsequent patches.
